project.title: VEVO Emo+: Unlocking Emotion Expressiveness in Voice Conversion with Emotion-Aware Prefix
project.subtitle: Anonymous Submission to Interspeech 2026
project.abstract: Recent advances in Voice Conversion (VC) have achieved remarkable speaker fidelity, yet repurposing these models for Emotional Voice Conversion (EVC) remains a challenge due to the risk of identity collapse. We propose VEVO Emo+, a framework that transforms a VC backbones into high-performance EVC systems with minimal architectural modification. By introducing Emotion-Aware Prefixes with Deep-Prefix Prompting, we achieve a significant breakthrough in affective precision, doubling the baseline Emotion Conversion Accuracy (ECA) from 42.40% to 85.50%. Our sensitivity analysis reveals a synergetic interaction between sequence-level prosody modulation and acoustic realization, where joint control at both stages is essential for categorical emotional distinctiveness. Furthermore, comparative analysis reveals that a decoupled acoustic realization stage acts as a critical identity anchor, enabling aggressive emotion conversion while maintaining the robust speaker fidelity and quality.

subset_note: Subset setting: 4 speakers, cross-emotion directions only, 4 candidate samples per speaker-direction (320 total candidates). The rows below are demo picks.

sections.title: Neutral to Emotional
sections.description: Source and target speakers are both seen during training.
